select-columns.label: Selecting Specific Columns
select-columns.summary: Learn how to retrieve only the columns you need.

sketch-what-is-select-columns.title: Why Select Specific Columns?
sketch-what-is-select-columns.bodyMarkdown: In SQL, you often don't need every column from a table. By specifying only the columns you want, you make your queries faster and your results easier to read. This is done by listing the column names after the `SELECT` keyword.

sketch-syntax-select-columns.title: Syntax for Selecting Columns
sketch-syntax-select-columns.bodyMarkdown:
To select specific columns, write their names separated by commas after `SELECT`. For example:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products_catalog;
```

This query returns only the `name` and `price` columns from the `products_catalog` table.

quiz-single-choice-select-columns.title: Choosing Columns in SELECT
quiz-single-choice-select-columns.prompt: Which SQL statement retrieves only the `name` and `price` columns from the `products_catalog` table?
quiz-single-choice-select-columns.hint: Look for the query that lists the column names after SELECT.

help.concept: To select specific columns, list them after the SELECT keyword and separate them with commas.
help.hint_1: The correct query should mention both 'name' and 'price' after SELECT.
help.hint_2: Remember to use commas between column names and specify the table after FROM.

quiz-multi-choice-why-select-columns.title: Benefits of Selecting Specific Columns
quiz-multi-choice-why-select-columns.prompt: Why might you want to select only specific columns in a SQL query? (Choose all that apply)
quiz-multi-choice-why-select-columns.hint: Think about readability, performance, and data size.

help.concept: Selecting only the columns you need can make results clearer and queries faster.
help.hint_1: Selecting fewer columns means less data is sent back.
help.hint_2: Selecting columns does not change the data in the table.

options.a: To make query results easier to read
options.b: To reduce the amount of data returned
options.c: To change the data in the table

quiz-drag-reorder-select-columns-syntax.title: Ordering the SELECT Statement
quiz-drag-reorder-select-columns-syntax.prompt: Arrange the parts of the SQL query to select the `name` and `price` columns from `products_catalog` in the correct order.
quiz-drag-reorder-select-columns-syntax.hint: Start with SELECT, then list columns, then FROM and the table name.

help.concept: The SELECT statement follows a specific order: SELECT, columns, FROM, table.
help.hint_1: The columns come right after SELECT.
help.hint_2: The table name comes after FROM.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-columns.title: Fill in the Blank: Selecting Columns
quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-columns.prompt:
Fill in the blank to select only the `name` column from the `products_catalog` table:

`SELECT ____ FROM products_catalog;`
quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-columns.hint: Which word should go in the blank to select just the 'the missing term' column?

help.concept: To select a single column, use its the missing term after SELECT.
help.hint_1: The blank should be filled with the column you want.
help.hint_2: You want only the 'the missing term' column, not all columns.

quiz-code-input-select-columns.title: Write a Query: Select Columns
quiz-code-input-select-columns.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the `product_id` and `price` columns from the `products_catalog` table.
quiz-code-input-select-columns.hint: List the two column names after SELECT, separated by a comma.

help.concept: To select multiple columns, list them after SELECT and separate with commas.
help.hint_1: The query should include both 'product_id' and 'price' after SELECT.
help.hint_2: Don't forget to specify the table after FROM.
